methodology: Semantic clustering of verbatim quotes by recurring patterns, emotional intensity, and linguistic markers

representative_quotes: P-042: starts perfectly straight and powerful, but as soon as the dimples catch the air it does an almost 90* turn to the right
P-021: if I swing as hard as I can, I could probably send the ball 2 fairways to the right
P-237: With driver it's in the adjacent fairway
P-001: I was always playing military golf (left, right, left, right)
P-044: I am always having to play from the right side
P-248: When I miss, I miss bad. I'm talking that I might only get 100-150 yards off the tee
P-247: Everything is extremely inconsistent

CLUSTER_1_SLICE_SUFFERING.sf2_relevance: DIRECT - SF2 positioned as slice-fixing driver

representative_quotes: P-046: mentally I am prepared to hit a bad shot before I even step up to the ball
P-221: I have lost all ability to hit the driver or any woods
V-109: I can easily feel a nice swing during my practice swings with the driver/woods but put me over a ball and well........disaster
P-221: This has occured more or less within the last 3-4 months
P-214: I used to hit that thing further, now I can't

CLUSTER_2_CONFIDENCE_DESTRUCTION.sf2_relevance: HIGH - Confidence restoration is key benefit

representative_quotes: P-043: I am constantly playing from behind other players in my group
P-216: He was always faaaarrr longer than me, these days he's practically playing the next hole before I catch up
P-225: People constantly make comments which they think are helpful but in fact have the opposite effect
P-226: The guys I was playing with tried to hide their frustrations with my 200 yard tee shot but I could sense it
P-241: 250 + yard drives with the equipment of the day was nothing spectacular - it was the norm for me. distance was gone, I mean G-O-N-E gone
P-219: I long wistfully for the days when I could carry that fairway bunker at 250 yards
P-243: it seems once I hit like 40 my distance took a hit, 45 a bigger hit and 50 a pretty damn big one
P-242: So it's been a considerable decline in 13 years
P-231: Every year when I change / buy new drivers, they're always 10 yards longer but here's the thing...each year as I get older, I lose 10 yards per year

CLUSTER_4_DISTANCE_DECLINE.sf2_relevance: HIGH - SF2 promises distance without sacrifice

representative_quotes: H-164: HUUUUUUGE difference to my game. Now with my C/F'd FT-5 and Aldila NVS shaft I am carrying about 220-230
H-153: Changed my ability to drive it in play 95% of time
H-168: The 2 things that were far and away the most important were the driver shaft fitting and the iron lie angle adjustment
RC-109: Most driver heads these days produce similar distance numbers. IMO, the right shaft is more important
H-170: I will never buy an off the shelf driver again

CLUSTER_5_FITTING_REVELATION.sf2_relevance: CRITICAL - SF2 needs to deliver this transformation

representative_quotes: H-160: WOW I never thought i'd lose my slice but its gone. My ball flies very straight about 85% of the time now
H-145: I rarely lose a drive right these days, certainly no power fades in evidence
H-146: Source: have used a G410 SFT for the last three years
CM-096: The Ping G series SFT are about as draw bias as you can get

CLUSTER_6_DRAW_BIAS_SUCCESS.sf2_relevance: DIRECT - SF2 is draw bias mechanism

CLUSTER_7_LESSONS_FAILED.summary: Multiple lessons and tips did not fix the fundamental problem
CLUSTER_7_LESSONS_FAILED.representative_quote: P-227: I've had numerous lessons over the years and nothing fixed my slice

CLUSTER_8_EQUIPMENT_SKEPTICISM.summary: Doubters who believe swing matters more than equipment
CLUSTER_8_EQUIPMENT_SKEPTICISM.representative_quote: V-116: A club will not fix you. If you want a band aid fix try closing the face

CLUSTER_9_3WOOD_ALTERNATIVE.representative_quote: H-171: In the last 3 rounds, I'm 80% on fairways with total yardage of around 235 yards

CLUSTER_10_SHORTER_SHAFT_SOLUTION.summary: Cutting shaft length as accuracy solution
CLUSTER_10_SHORTER_SHAFT_SOLUTION.representative_quote: ST-114: I'm playing a 44 inch Mizuno ST-190G. Now it's just an inch longer than a typical 3 wood

sf2_positioning_implications.primary_benefit_message: Fix your slice without sacrificing distance
secondary_messages: Stop the embarrassment on the course
No more lessons that don't work
Feel what a fitted driver should feel like
